Stages.CompletionOutcome: Speaker reaches Elementary Speaking (A1–A2) Comfortable speaking 60 minutes continuously Fear of speaking largely removed
Stages.Descriptions: CEFR-aligned (Cambridge A1 → early A2) topic framework for STAGE 1 – BEGINNER (Levels 1–30) of LISA, focusing ONLY on SPEAKING, designed for gamification, daily-life relevance, and psychological safety for shy or low-confidence speakers.

Stages.CompletionOutcome: Stable A2+ → B1- speaking Can speak 90 minutes continuously Can explain, compare, give reasons
Stages.Descriptions: This practice will NOT repeat Stage 1 mechanics, only what is new and expanded, so this is easy for your team to plug into the system. Below is a complete, systematic sub-topic breakdown for STAGE 2 (Levels 31–60), using 15-minute blocks, fully aligned with A2+ → B1- speaking (Cambridge CEFR) and 100% speaking-only for LISA gamification.

SubLevel.MainTask: My full name, My nickname, How people call me
Country, City, One simple fact
Student / worker, Simple sentence: “I am…”
I like…, I don’t like…
Ask and answer with partners
“My name is… I’m from… I am…”
Talk about a normal weekday from morning to night.(What time you wake up, work/study, rest)
Compare what you do on weekends vs weekdays.
Describe a busy day and a free day.Say which one you prefer and why (simple reasons).
Explain how you balance work/study and rest.
Talk about changes in your routine recently.
Tell the story of one full week in your life.
Describe your morning routine and how you use time.
Talk about the busiest time of your day.
Share experiences of being late or early.
Explain how you plan your day (or don’t).
Talk about problems with time (too busy, tired).
Explain one habit you want to improve.
Describe what you eat in a normal day.
Talk about eating times, snacks, drinks.
Compare eating at home and eating outside.
Give examples of healthy and unhealthy habits.
Talk about changes in your eating habits.
Explain how food affects your daily life.
Describe places where you work or study.
Talk about cafes or restaurants you like.
Describe where you usually shop.
Talk about places where you relax.
Explain why you like these places.
Describe your daily movement between places.
Talk about people you meet every day.
Describe your friends or classmates.
Explain how often you talk to family.
Talk about your neighbors or people nearby.
Describe how you communicate with people.
Summarize who is important in your daily life.
Tell a positive experience from the past.
Describe a difficult or unpleasant experience.
Talk about doing something for the first time.
Explain what you learned from that experience.
Compare how you felt then and now.
Tell the full story clearly.
Describe how you travel daily.
Talk about problems with transport.
Explain how you prefer to travel and why.
Describe a trip you took before.
Talk about a trip you want to take.
Tell a complete travel story.
Describe what you study or learned before.
Explain how you usually study.
Talk about difficulties in learning.
Compare studying alone vs with others.
Explain how you want to improve.
Reflect on your learning journey.
Describe your main daily tasks.
Talk about responsibilities at work/study.
Explain tasks you find easy.
Describe tasks you find difficult.
Talk about teamwork experiences.
Describe one full work/study day.
Talk about problems speaking or understanding.
Describe a misunderstanding.
Practice asking people to repeat or explain.
Explain how you clarify information.
Talk about solutions.
Tell a communication problem story.

SubLevel.SublevelTitle: Things I like in daily life
SubLevel.MainTask: Talk about activities, food, places, or habits you like.
Describe things you don’t enjoy in daily life.
Explain why you like or dislike something (because, so).
Talk about something you liked before but not now (or vice versa).
Compare two things you like or dislike (A vs B).
Summarize what your likes say about you.
Talk about choices you make every day (food, clothes, time).
Describe choices related to study or work.
Explain how you choose A or B.
Talk about good and bad points of a choice.
Describe a choice you feel good or bad about.
Tell the story of one decision from start to result.
Describe how you use your phone every day.
Talk about apps or tools you often use.
Explain how technology helps your life.
Describe problems or stress caused by technology.
Talk about good or bad tech habits.
Summarize how technology affects your life.
Describe movies or series you watch.
Talk about music you like and when you listen.
Describe YouTube, TikTok, games, or online content.
Explain how often and when you relax with media.
Compare entertainment alone vs with friends.
Tell a story about a memorable entertainment experience.
Describe what and how often you shop.
Compare shopping online and in stores.
Talk about how you choose products.
Describe good or bad service experiences.
Talk about problems and how you solved them.
Tell a full shopping experience story.
Describe how you meet new people.
Talk about common small talk topics.
Explain how you continue a conversation.
Describe your feelings in social situations.
Talk about problems in social communication.
Tell a story about a social situation.
Describe tasks you do in a group.
Explain your role when working with others.
Talk about problems in teamwork.
Practice sharing opinions politely.
Explain how problems were solved.
Summarize what you learned about teamwork.
Describe problems at work, study, or home.
Explain why these problems happen.
Talk about how you ask for help.
Suggest simple ways to solve problems.
Explain what happened after solving the problem.
Tell a full problem → solution story.
Describe when and why you feel tired.
Talk about things that cause stress.
Explain what helps you relax.
Describe habits for better health.
Talk about balancing work/study and rest.
Summarize your health and balance situation.
Describe plans for this week or month.
Talk about plans that changed.
Explain how you plan with friends or colleagues.
Describe plans that are hard to make.
Talk about success or failure of plans.
Summarize your planning style.
Describe your life in the past.
Talk about your current life.
Describe one important life change.
Talk about small changes in habits.
Compare good and bad changes.
Tell the story of a life change.
Describe goals in your personal life.
Talk about career or study goals.
Explain what motivates you.
Talk about problems in reaching goals.
Describe how you keep going.
Summarize your goals and motivation.
Compare your life with others’.
Compare different living styles.
Compare ways of working or studying.
Compare your past and present life.
Explain which you prefer and why.
Summarize your comparisons.
Describe common problems people have.
Give advice using should / can.
Talk about advice you gave or received.
Compare helpful and unhelpful advice.
Talk about results of following advice.
Simulate giving advice in a situation.
Describe a problem step by step.
Explain why it happened.
Suggest different solutions.
Explain why you chose one solution.
Describe the outcome.
Tell the full problem-solving story.
Talk about a positive work/study experience.
Describe a challenging situation.
Talk about mistakes you made.
Describe people in that experience.
Explain how you changed.
Summarize the experience.
Share opinions on work and study.
Compare two lifestyles.
Talk about modern life.
Explain advantages and disadvantages.
Share your preference.
Summarize your views.
Explain how you do something daily.
Describe steps of a simple process.
Support ideas with examples.
Practice making ideas clear.
Ask if others understand.
Explain something from start to end.
Talk about future directions.
Describe skills you want to improve.
Talk about future difficulties.
Explain how you prepare.
Share hopes and worries.
Describe your future life.
Talk about where you started.
Describe your current life.
Talk about what you do well.
Describe difficulties you face.
Talk about where you are going.
